qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated low-privilege user can restart the Ajenti master process
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about unauthorized custom package installation through Ajenti's task-launch path and plugin install tasks. This finding instead targets `POST /api/core/restart-master` in `plugins/core/views/api.py`, which is a different endpoint, sink, and root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Custom-user fs_root confinement is not enforced on the filesystem read API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory concerns the task/package-install control flow in `plugins/core/views/tasks.py` and `plugins/plugins/tasks.py`. This finding is a filesystem boundary failure in `plugins/filesystem/views.py` and does not touch the advisory's package-install logic.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's vulnerable task-launch and custom-package installation path. The advisory root cause is the missing restriction around `POST /api/core/tasks/start` plus package/plugin install task execution, while the accepted session findings are a restart-master authorization flaw and an `fs_root` filesystem-read boundary bypass.
evidence: The advisory summary states that an authenticated `auth_users` user could install a custom package even when not superuser.
Pre-fix `plugins/core/views/tasks.py` accepts attacker-controlled `cls` values at `POST /api/core/tasks/start` and instantiates the requested task class without the later task allowlist.
Pre-fix `plugins/plugins/tasks.py` runs `InstallPlugin`, `UnInstallPlugin`, and `UpgradeAll` without the later `os.getuid()` privilege checks introduced by the task hardening commits.
The session's qualifying findings instead target `POST /api/core/restart-master` and `GET /api/filesystem/read/<path>`, so neither report reaches the advisory's package-install source-to-sink path.
